title: melica ciliata subsp. klokovii
intro: Melica ciliata subsp. klokovii, also known as the Russian melic or a subspecies of the melic grass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, and is often used to treat coughs, colds, and other illnesses. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to be used as a remedy, and it is also sometimes used in folk medicine in Eastern Europe and Russia.
